attributes.readMe:
## Popover

An anchored floating surface that hosts a focused interaction next to a source element — it **raises** a focused something out of the source's small footprint without taking the user away from the source. A standalone, remixable extraction of the `boxel-surface` `Lift` primitive whose only npm dependency is `@floating-ui/dom` (shimmed as a global virtual module in every realm).

### Orthogonal axes
Each dial does exactly one thing and reads only its own value, so any combination is predictable:
- **kind** — `details` · `edit` · `tools` — ARIA role + autofocus default + CSS theme
- **anchoring** — `beside` · `overlay` · `center` — position only
- **size** — `compact` · `comfortable` · `spacious` · `auto` — min/max width + height
- **backdrop** — `none` · `tint` · `blur` · `dim` — surface material
- **elevation** — `flat` · `raised` · `elevated` · `floating` — shadow depth + radius

### Keyboard / focus (explicit opt-ins)
- **@keyboardModel** — `'pick'` (focus a `[role=listbox]`, route arrow keys) or `'edit'` (focus the editor input, route typing keys). Omit to leave key handling to the host.
- **@trapFocus** — trap Tab inside the popover (aria-modal). Use for editor popovers that own the full focus cycle.

### Escalation
Pass `@canEscalateTo` (kinds other than the current `@kind`) plus `@onEscalate` to render a compact corner glyph (✎ → edit, ⓘ → details, ⋯ → tools / multiple targets). The toolbar is OFF by default; single-kind contracts get no chrome.

### Calling convention
The host owns **what** (open/close state, kind state, body content); the Popover owns **how** (positioning, dismissal on Esc / outside-click, focus enter/restore, per-kind chrome, the optional dim overlay).

## Usage

The **Popover Playground** example is the canonical reference — every axis is a live control there, and it prints the exact invocation as you change dials. The minimal shape:

```gts
import Popover from '../popover.gts';

<template>
  <button data-anchor='preview'>Open</button>

  <Popover
    @anchor='[data-anchor=preview]'
    @open={{this.open}}
    @kind='details'
    @anchoring='beside'
    @placement='bottom'
    @offset={{8}}
    @size='comfortable'
    @backdrop='none'
    @elevation='raised'
    @onDismiss={{this.close}}
  >
    …your content…
  </Popover>
</template>
```

- `@anchor` is a CSS selector (or element) the popover positions against.
- `@open` + `@onDismiss` — the host owns open/close state; the popover calls `@onDismiss` on Esc / outside-click.
- `@placement` / `@offset` / `@arrow` apply only when `@anchoring='beside'`.
- For an editor popover, add `@kind='edit'`, `@keyboardModel='edit'`, and `@trapFocus={{true}}`.
- For hover-to-open / click-to-escalate wiring across many source units, drive it through `PopoverState` + the `surfacePopoverBinding` modifier rather than managing `@open` by hand.

## Companion modules (bundled in this listing)
- **surfacePopoverBinding** (`modifiers/popover-binding`) — element modifier that translates pointer/click gestures on each source unit into `PopoverState` transitions.
- **PopoverState** (`utils/popover-state`) — per-host state machine owning the open `(row, col, kind)` plus hover-pause / dismiss-grace timers.
- **SurfaceLayerManager** (`utils/layer-manager`) — dynamic z-index allocator so nested popovers / lifts / modals stack deterministically.
- **popover-types** (`utils/popover-types`) — the axis unions plus kind→glyph / kind→label maps and the escalation-priority resolver.
attributes.cardDescription: An anchored floating surface that raises a focused interaction next to its source element. Five orthogonal axes (kind, anchoring, size, backdrop, elevation) plus opt-in keyboard delegation, focus-trapping, and kind escalation.
